@comment: 1. VNIR — Planet Labs SuperDove PSB.SD
Model: VNIR (Aslan15 fork — VNIRSensorModel)
Sensor: 8-band VNIR pushbroom frame imager, 3.7–4.2 m GSD, ~24 km swath
Not steerable — nadir-fixed strip imager
VNIR_no_steer.name: Planet SuperDove PSB.SD
@notes: detectorWidth and focalLength chosen to satisfy IFOV = d/f = 3.3e-6/0.042 ≈ 7.9e-5 rad.
At 500 km altitude: GSD = IFOV * h = 7.9e-5 * 500e3 ≈ 3.95 m (consistent with 3.7–4.2 m spec).
FOV = numberDetectorCols * IFOV = 6144 * 7.9e-5 rad ≈ 2.7° cross-track.
8 spectral bands: coastal blue (431–452 nm), blue (465–515 nm), green I (513–549 nm),
green (547–583 nm), red (650–680 nm), yellow (600–620 nm), red edge (697–713 nm), NIR (845–885 nm).
Frame imager (MATRIX_IMAGER scan in PassiveOpticalScanner terms). No maneuver — nadir-fixed.

@comment: 2. VNIR — Planet Labs Pelican Gen-1
Model: Passive Optical Scanner
Sensor: TDI pushbroom, 0.5 m GSD, 8 km swath, f=4.03 m, 12288-px PAN
Steerable: ±agile slew (modelled as Circular maneuver ±15°)
VNIR_agile.name: Planet Pelican Gen-1 Imager
@notes: Focal length 4.03 m, pixel pitch ~4.24 µm (back-calculated: GSD=0.5m at 475km → p=0.5*4.03/475e3).
F# = f/D → D = 4.03/11.5 ≈ 0.35 m aperture diameter (estimated).
FOV angleHeight set to 0.1° (single TDI line at any instant; scene collected along-track).
FOV angleWidth = 2*arctan(12288*4.24e-6 / (2*4.03)) ≈ 0.74° cross-track.
sceneFOV angleHeight = 10° represents a typical long along-track collect strip.
sceneFOV angleWidth = 0.97° matches the published 8 km product swath at 475 km.
Maneuver: Circular 30° diameter represents ±15° 3D agility at 15°/s slew rate.
6 MS bands: Blue(465–518nm), Green(547–585nm), Red(650–682nm), RE(699–716nm),
NIR-Wide(779–885nm), NIR-Narrow(846–887nm). PAN: 450–800nm.
operatingWavelength and bandwidth approximate PAN band center and width.

@comment: 3. SAR — Capella Space X-SAR
Model: Synthetic Aperture Radar
X-band, 9.4–9.9 GHz, 500–700 MHz chirp BW, spotlight 0.25 m / stripmap 1.2 m
Steerable: Double_Roll_Only (left-look AND right-look, 15°–55° each side)
SAR.name: Capella X-SAR

mode.@notes: 0.25 m spotlight, 5x5 km scene. spotlight beam-steering modelled via fixed small swath.

@notes: operatingFrequency: center of 9.4–9.9 GHz band, set to 9.65 GHz.
chirpBandwidth: 700 MHz (maximum); 500 MHz in stripmap mode.
pulseWidth: 25 µs (estimated from 15% duty cycle at 6 kHz PRF).
apertureHeight (elevation/range): 3.3 m (back-calculated: 8 m² / 2.4 m).
apertureWidth (azimuth/along-track): 2.4 m (back-calculated: δaz = L/2 = 1.2 m).
minimumPRF: 6333 Hz (2*vs/L = 2*7600/2.4).
maximumPRF: 30000 Hz (spotlight mode, tight 5 km dwell window).
maneuver: Double_Roll_Only models bilateral side-look (left and right of track),
A: right-look 15°–55°, B: left-look −55° to −15°. Nadir exclusion preserved.
peakTransmitPower: 2000 W (2 kW, consistent with ICEYE/Capella class).
systemNoiseFigure: 4 dB (typical X-band T/R module).
radarLosses: 3.5 dB (typical microwave path + feed losses).
atmosLoss: 0.5 dB (2-way, dry atmosphere at X-band).

@comment: 4. TIR — constellr HiVE SkyBee-1
Model: Passive Optical Scanner (best fit for cryocooled pushbroom LWIR imager)
LWIR 8–12 µm, 4 bands, 28.9 m GSD, 18.5 km swath, MCT 640×512 detector
Steerable: Single_Roll_Only (±30° off-nadir tasking)
TIR.name: constellr HiVE TIR Imager (SkyBee-1)
@notes: Instrument type: Passive Optical Scanner used as best available model for TIR pushbroom.
The SNR calculation will use thermal radiance (blackbody target) rather than solar reflectance.
Detector: MCT 640×512 pixels. Here numberDetectorCols=640 (cross-track, active pixels).
detectorWidth: 15 µm (typical MCT ROIC pitch for 640×512 format).
focalLength estimated: GSD=28.9m at 510km → IFOV=28.9/510e3=5.67e-5 rad → f=p/IFOV=15e-6/5.67e-5≈0.265 m.
NOTE: revised to f=0.095 m to match 2.08° cross-track FOV with 640 pixels:
FOV=640*15e-6/0.095 = 0.1008 rad = 5.78° — this is the raw detector array FOV.
angleWidth 2.08° is set to match the 18.5 km published swath at 510 km.
Users should treat focalLength and detectorWidth as consistent relative to FOV, not absolute.
operatingWavelength: 10 µm (center of 8–12 µm LWIR band).
bandwidth: 4 µm (full 8–12 µm LWIR window).
NEDT: 30 mK (published). L2 LST accuracy: <2.0 K.
Detector cooled to ~70 K (cryocooled MCT — not modelled explicitly in instrupy).
maneuver: Single_Roll_Only ±30° models the published off-nadir pointing capability.
4 spectral bands within 8–12 µm (band centers not publicly disclosed).
Daytime AND night-time capable (passive thermal emission, no solar illumination required).

@comment: 5. MIR — OroraTech SAFIRE Gen2 / OTC-P1
Model: Basic Sensor (no specific MIR model in instrupy)
3-channel TIR (1× MWIR ~3–5 µm + 2× LWIR ~8–12 µm), 200 m GSD, ~400 km swath
Not steerable — wide-angle nadir-pointing fire surveillance
MIR.name: OroraTech OTC-P1 SAFIRE Thermal Camera
@notes: Model: Basic Sensor — no MIR-specific model exists in instrupy (including fork).
FOV: 2*arctan(400,000/(2*525,000)) ≈ 41.7° — derived from 400 km swath at 525 km altitude.
Native GSD: ~200 m/pixel. AI-enhanced hotspot detection to ~4 m (algorithmic, not optical).
3 spectral channels: 1× MWIR (3–5 µm, high-temp flames) + 2× LWIR (8–12 µm, smoldering).
Uncooled detector (passive thermal stabilisation — no cryocooler).
Thermal sensitivity: 0.5°C NETD. Absolute temperature accuracy: ±1°C.
Platform: 8U CubeSat on Spire LEMUR bus (~20×20×30 cm).
Onboard Nvidia Jetson GPU for real-time fire detection — fire alerts within 3 min of detection.
Swath: ~400 km (very wide by design for surveillance, not imaging).
Day AND night capable (passive thermal emission sensing).
Additional RGB context camera on same satellite (not modelled here — separate instrument spec).
numberDetectorRows/Cols: 512×512 approximate (exact detector array size not publicly disclosed).

@comment: 7. Radar Altimeter — CNES/LEGOS SMASH / Blue Water Intelligence Ka-band nadir
Model: Altimeter (Aslan15 fork — AltimeterSensorModel)
Ka-band nadir pulse-limited, 35 GHz, 3200 MHz BW, 10 cm height accuracy
Not steerable — pure nadir altimeter (point measurement below satellite)
Altimeter.name: SMASH Ka-band Nadir Altimeter (BWI/CNES)
@notes: Model: Altimeter (Aslan15 fork AltimeterSensorModel — extends BasicSensorModel with
chirpBandwidth and pulseWidth parameters).
Technique: nadir pulse-limited Ka-band radar altimeter. Frequency: ~35.75 GHz.
chirpBandwidth: 3200 MHz (published). Height resolution ≈ c/(2*B) = 3e8/(2*3.2e9) ≈ 4.7 cm.
pulseWidth: 1/(2*B) = 1/(2*3.2e9) ≈ 0.31 ns (compressed pulse width; uncompressed longer).
Height accuracy: 10 cm (end-to-end, including POD contribution).
Radial POD requirement: 5 cm (met via bi-frequency GNSS receiver, not modelled here).
Antenna: ~35 cm parabolic dish. Transmitted power: <0.5 W (efficient due to high water σ₀).
fieldOfViewGeometry diameter: 0.5° approximates the nadir beam footprint
(pulse-limited circle ~few km; angular equivalent at 500 km altitude ≈ 0.5°).
Coverage model: virtual stations at track × water-body intersections (~30,000 globally).
NOT a swath imager — coverage is 1D along ground tracks only.
Min river width detectable: 50 m. Min lake size: 100×100 m.
10-nanosatellite constellation on single SSO plane provides daily continental water coverage.
Day AND night, all-weather capable.
